description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with dark shingles and a prominent overhanging eave. The front facade includes a covered porch supported by wooden posts, with a wooden panel enclosing one side. The exterior walls are finished in a light-colored material, likely stucco or painted concrete, and the windows are framed in wood, with two rectangular windows on the upper story. The roof extends over the porch, providing shelter and architectural interest. The house sits on a brick or tile patio that extends around the porch area. The overall design is simple and functional, with a focus on practicality and modest decorative elements.
description: A gable-roofed house with a covered porch, light exterior walls, and wood-framed windows, set on a brick patio. The design is simple and functional with a prominent overhanging roof.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, wooden posts, and two upper-story windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steep gable roof and the depth of the porch overhang.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying two large windows and the plain, unadorned back wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the roof's full length and the wooden panel enclosing part of the porch.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with wooden posts, partial wooden enclosure, and a simple main door under a roof overhang
